whatIs.title: What Is an Equestrian Simulator?
whatIs.desc: An equestrian simulator is an AI-powered riding training device that replicates a horse's gait and movement through precision sensors and biomechanical algorithms — enabling riders to train effectively in a zero-risk environment. Widely adopted by equestrian clubs, schools, rehabilitation centres and professional jockey training programmes as the ideal complement to live horse training.

items.quote: The MS.30P has significantly advanced our jockey training programme. The precision sensor feedback provides biomechanical insights we simply cannot obtain through conventional training methods.
items.name: Head of Training

items.quote: Safety is paramount for our disabled riders. The MS.30's emergency stop and gentle gait simulation create a truly zero-risk environment, enabling our participants to build confidence at their own pace.
items.name: Programme Manager
items.org: Riding for the Disabled Association, Hong Kong

items.quote: Since introducing the simulator, our equestrian programme capacity has tripled. Students train regardless of weather or horse availability, and learning outcomes have improved markedly.
items.name: Head of Equestrian Studies

items.quote: The simulator gives our students a solid foundation before they ever ride a real horse. Parents appreciate the safety, and enrolment in our equestrian programme has grown by 40%.
items.name: Training Director
items.org: Sun Yat-sen University Shenzhen Affiliated School

cta.title: Ready to Transform Your Training?
cta.subtitle: Speak with our team for a personalised consultation and product demonstration.

items.q: How does a simulator compare to training on a real horse?
items.a: Our simulators replicate equine biomechanics via a 6-axis motion platform and 200+ precision sensors, delivering consistent, measurable training conditions. Unlike live horses, the simulator is unaffected by the animal's mood, weather or venue availability — making it the ideal complement to real horse training.

items.q: Who is the equestrian simulator suitable for?
items.a: Suitable for all riders: beginners building fundamental seat and balance; advanced riders refining technical skills; rehabilitation patients recovering safely; and professional jockeys training weight distribution and reaction response.

items.q: Which models do you offer?
items.a: We offer three models: MS.30P (professional eventing grade), MS.30 / Pony-MS.30 (education and rehabilitation grade, both CE certified), and MS.20 (specialist jockey and racehorse training). Contact us for a recommendation tailored to your needs.

items.q: How do I request a quote?
items.a: Complete the enquiry form on our Contact page, or email us directly at info@equestrian-simulators.com. We aim to respond within 24 hours with a customised proposal.

ms30p.subtitle: Professional eventing simulator built for elite competition and advanced training

immersive.title: High-Precision Biomechanical Sensing
immersive.desc: High-sensitivity sensor arrays at the horse head and abdomen capture rider weight shifts and leg aid commands in real time, with rein tension sensing delivering millisecond dynamic response.

features.title: Core Features
features.subtitle: Professional-grade technology engineered for competitive training

card1.title: Intelligent Aid Recognition
card1.desc: Recognises inside-leg aids and triggers precise gait transitions and speed changes through real biomechanical motion, supporting advanced movements including Piaffe and Passage.

card2.title: Full Eventing Experience
card2.desc: Accurately reproduces walk, trot and canter, supporting advanced dressage movements including Piaffe and Passage, plus show jumping course simulation.

card3.title: Professional Safety System
card3.desc: Dual protection with emergency brake and software fault guard. CE (RED/EMC/LVD) certified. Officially adopted by Hong Kong Jockey Club Shenzhen Centre.

whatIs.title: What is the MS.30P Professional Equestrian Simulator?
whatIs.desc: The MS.30P is a professional equestrian simulator designed for elite competition and advanced training. Powered by an AI biomechanical feedback system, it is officially endorsed by the Hong Kong Jockey Club (HKJC) and CE (RED/EMC/LVD) certified. Equipped with 200+ precision sensors, it accurately reproduces walk, trot and canter, and supports advanced dressage movements including Piaffe and Passage, as well as show jumping course simulation.

items.q: What age group is the MS.30P suitable for?
items.a: The MS.30P is designed for riders aged 10 and above, particularly intermediate to advanced youth and professional adult riders. The Piaffe and Passage functions are ideal for riders with competitive ambitions. For children under 10, we recommend the MS.30 education model.

items.q: What site conditions are required for installation?
items.a: Minimum ceiling height >=3000mm, standard 220V/4.8KW power supply. Machine dimensions: 2263x1122x1750mm, weight 450KG, rated load 98KG. Side clearance >=300mm, front/rear clearance >=500mm. Door width >=1222mm (assembled), >=800mm (disassembled).

items.q: How does simulator training differ from riding a real horse?
items.a: The MS.30P uses a 6-axis motion platform and 200+ sensors to reproduce the biomechanics of a real horse, including advanced gaits like Piaffe and Passage. While it cannot replicate each horse individual character, it delivers stable, measurable training conditions - the ideal complement to real horse training.

items.q: What training data does the AI system provide?
items.a: After each session, a report is automatically generated covering weight balance distribution, posture scoring, Piaffe/Passage gait assessment and training progress trends, all exportable.

items.q: What does after-sales service and installation include?
items.a: The MS.30P comes with a 1-year full-machine warranty including one free on-site installation and staff training. The base is fitted with caster wheels for movement on flat surfaces.

features.title: Core Features

card1.title: Dual Instruction Modes

card2.title: Three-Gait Simulation

specs.title: Technical Specifications
left: Riding for the Disabled Association (RDA) Hong Kong

whatIs.title: What is the MS.30 Smart Equestrian Simulator?
whatIs.desc: The MS.30 is an education-grade equestrian simulator for riding schools, universities and rehabilitation centres. Multiple units connect over LAN, giving instructors real-time visibility of every rider from a central dashboard. Officially recognised by the Riding for the Disabled Association (RDA) Hong Kong and CE (RED/EMC/LVD) certified. It accurately simulates Walk, Trot and Canter with gentle, safe parameters suitable for children aged 4+ and adult beginners.

items.q: What age group is the MS.30 suitable for?
items.a: Suitable for all ages from 4 years old. Gait parameters are gentle and safe, ideal for children and beginners. For competitive training, consider upgrading to the MS.30P.

items.q: How does the multi-unit network work?
items.a: Multiple MS.30 units connect over a local area network. Instructors can view each rider's data, posture scores and training progress in real time from a central control dashboard.

items.q: Does installation require special venue modifications?
items.a: No special modifications needed. Requires standard 220V/4KW power. Machine dimensions: 2068x1002x1500mm, weight 300KG, rated load 80KG. Ceiling height >=2600mm, side clearance >=300mm, front/rear clearance >=500mm. Door width >=1102mm (assembled) or >=670mm (disassembled).

items.q: What is the difference between MS.30 and MS.30P?
items.a: The MS.30 is the education model with gentle gaits and multi-unit networking for group instruction. The MS.30P is the professional model with high-precision biomechanical sensors, supporting advanced dressage movements including Piaffe and Passage.

items.q: What does the warranty and after-sales service include?
items.a: 1-year full machine warranty including one free on-site installation and staff training session, 7x24 remote technical support, and free software upgrades within the warranty period.

ms20.subtitle: High-Speed Simulator Built for Jockeys and Racing Training

immersive.title: High-Speed Gallop Experience System
immersive.desc: Designed for racing training and exhibition experiences. A pneumatic saddle system precisely replicates jockey posture, supports high-speed gallop simulation, and pairs with a large-screen immersive display — the professional choice for jockey training and racehorse experiences.

features.title: Core Features
features.subtitle: Professional Racing — Built for Jockey Training

card1.title: High-Speed Gallop Simulation
card1.desc: Precisely simulates racehorse gallop movements with adjustable high-speed parameters. Riders build correct muscle memory for competitive racing in a safe, controlled environment.

card2.title: Pneumatic Saddle System
card2.desc: A unique pneumatic sensor saddle detects rider centre-of-gravity distribution and seat-bone pressure in real time, generating posture scores and helping jockeys optimise balance and power output.

card3.title: Lightweight Compact Design
card3.desc: At just 200KG, the MS.20 has a small footprint and can be flexibly deployed at jockey training centres, racecourses, exhibitions and experience events.

whatIs.title: What is the MS.20 Racing Equestrian Simulator?
whatIs.desc: The MS.20 is a racing-grade equestrian simulator designed for jockey training, racehorse experiences and exhibitions. Its pneumatic saddle system precisely reproduces high-speed gallop riding. At just 200KG, the compact design deploys flexibly at jockey training centres, racecourses and exhibition venues. Ideal for jockey training institutions, racehorse experience centres, exhibitions and amusement venues.

items.q: What is the difference between MS.20 and MS.30?
items.a: The MS.20 is the racing specialist model with a pneumatic saddle, focused on high-speed gallop simulation for jockey training and racehorse experiences. The MS.30 is the education model supporting Walk/Trot/Canter for group school instruction. The MS.30P is the professional model supporting advanced dressage movements.

items.q: Where is the MS.20 suitable for use?
items.a: Ideal for jockey training centres, racecourses, exhibition venues, experience centres and large events. At 200KG, the unit deploys flexibly without permanent installation.

items.q: How does the pneumatic saddle work?
items.a: The saddle houses an array of pneumatic pressure sensors that detect seat-bone pressure distribution and centre-of-gravity shifts in real time. The system generates posture scores to help jockeys optimise their balance and power delivery.

items.q: What do I need to know about installation and transport?
items.a: No special venue modifications required. Needs standard 220V/1.2KW power. Machine dimensions: 2000x600x1610mm, weight 200KG, rated load 80KG. Ceiling height >=2600mm, door width >=700mm (assembled). Base includes castors for movement on flat surfaces.

items.q: What does the warranty and after-sales service include?
items.a: 1-year full machine warranty including one free on-site installation and staff training, 7x24 remote technical support, and free software upgrades within the warranty period.

items.q: What is the difference between MS.30P and MS.30?
items.a: The MS.30P is professional eventing grade with high-precision biomechanical sensors and leg aid recognition, ideal for professional riders and elite clubs. The MS.30 is the education and rehabilitation grade for schools and rehab centres. Both are CE certified.

items.q: Are the simulators CE certified?
items.a: The MS.30P and MS.30 are both CE certified (RED/EMC/LVD), meeting EU safety standards for global markets. The MS.20 does not currently hold CE certification.

items.q: How do I choose the right model for my institution?
items.a: Choose by use case: MS.30P for professional training and elite clubs; MS.30 for schools and rehabilitation centres; MS.20 for jockey training and racehorse experiences. Contact us for tailored advice.

items.q: Do the simulators support multi-unit networked training?
items.a: Both the MS.30P and MS.30 support multi-unit networking with unlimited units, allowing instructors to monitor all riders in real time. The MS.20 does not currently support networking.
